role: The Wheel is the core polar/spatial instrument view that renders a projection as an animated SVG: sectors, point cloud, structural-meaning strain, directed sector edges, scale rungs, and addressable hit targets.
capabilities: Responsive SVG wheel geometry from container size
Dyadic square grid, axes, and forbidden corner circle overlay
Concentric rings and coincidence-spine diamonds on axial ring crossings
Sector wedges with per-sector hue, selection/dimming, and click addressing
Directed and bidirectional sector edge chords with arrowheads and selection emphasis
Radial strain lines showing structural-vs-semantic filing tension per point
Animated decorative point cloud with entrance/exit/tween and selected-point stroke
Theme name labels for coarse-scale cluster points when count is small
Invisible 15px hit layer over points to capture taps and invoke onPick
Center origin mark and optional center ring
Scale rung ladder via RungLadder when setRung is provided
Connection readout with in/out counts and pure source/sink findings
Address stamping for regions, sectors, edges, points, and readout
substance_why: The file implements a complete, multi-layer polar visualization with grid, rings, sectors, edges, strain, points, labels, rung controls, readout, and interaction wiring rather than stubs.
interactions_present: Sector click toggles selection, dims other sectors, emphasizes connected edges, and dispatches a window projection:aim custom event
Invisible point hit layer calls onPick(p) when a point is clicked
RungLadder calls setRung to change scale rung
Selected point receives a visible stroke highlight
interactions_missing: No keyboard navigation or shortcuts for sectors/points
No hover tooltips or preview for points or edges
No zoom, pan, or touch gestures on the wheel
No point label or detail shown on selection beyond stroke
No listener for projection:aim inside this file; integration with the right-hand panel is not visible
No empty/error state for malformed or zero-point projections
No multi-select, lasso, or drag interactions
performance_risks: Point positions, addresses, and theme names are recomputed in multiple passes over proj.points per render
Framer-motion layoutId and enter/exit animations are applied to every point without virtualization
Edge readout filters all edges twice per render
Inline IIFEs and closures are recreated on every render inside JSX
SVG viewBox uses actual pixel dimensions, so a large container produces a large DOM coordinate space
reuse_why: The polar-projection semantics, address stamping, and interaction contracts are domain-specific and should survive, but the monolithic SVG render and repeated computations need a structured rebuild.
decomposition_hints: Extract subcomponents for Grid, Rings, Sectors, Edges, Strain, Points, Labels, HitLayer, and ConnectionReadout
Introduce memoized hooks for wheel geometry, sector selection state, edge role classification, and point positions
Separate the invisible interaction target layer from decorative rendering
Isolate the animation/feel wrapper so motion behavior can be tuned independently
Move repeated pure calculations such as point addresses and theme names into prepared projection data or memoized selectors
